title: Montana Renewables to Add 150 MMgy of SAF Capacity by 2026
content:
Calumet Inc. reported third-quarter results and shared updates on the MaxSAF initiative by Montana Renewables, supported by a conditional loan guarantee from the U.S. Department of Energy of up to $1.44 billion. This funding will aid in expanding Montana Renewablesâ€™ biorefinery in Great Falls, Montana, aiming to make it one of the worldâ€™s largest producers of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), with a projected capacity of 300 million gallons per year (MMgy) for SAF and 330 MMgy for combined SAF and renewable diesel.

Calumetâ€™s CEO, Todd Borgmann, highlighted the modular approach of the MaxSAF project, with the first phase expected to be completed by 2026, increasing SAF production to 150 MMgy. Future expansions will involve further infrastructure improvements, including renewable hydrogen production, feedstock pretreatment, a wastewater system, and cogeneration for renewable electricity and steam, as well as enhanced truck-loading capabilities for SAF.
author: Analysts Account
